timeline.details: We are currently investigating the issue for the instability
Stability for Start Workflow and Task Retrieval has been restored. The engineering team will continue monitoring the services closely.
System stability has been restored, and all systems have remained stable after a day of monitoring.
We are currently investigating an issue where customers may not be able to connect to the Nintex App Studio website.
We have identified the root cause and applied a mitigation. Access to the site has now been restored.
We have identified the root cause and applied a mitigation. Access to the site has now been restored.

incidents.title: Nintex Automation Cloud file service is unavailable due to ongoing outage on Azure Service Bus.

timeline.details:
We are currently investigating an issue caused by an Microsoft Azure Service degredation.

This issue may may present as:

 1. Workflows taking  longer to start than normal
2. File related actions may error
3. Forms with attachments may not submit
4. Workflow Alerting may not 

Our engineering team is currently evaluating failover options and will update the status as more information is available.

Microsoft have confirmed that there is currently a service degradation impacting Azure Service Bus in several regions globally which MS are actively mitigating.

This is impacting a number of capabilities within Nintex Workflow including - Workflow Alerting, File Attachments in Forms, Tasks, & Activity Feed.

Our engineering team is in constant contact with MS Support and will update this post when more information is avaiable.

Microsoft has applied mitigation to the service degradation impacting Azure Service Bus. The recovery has made the impacting Nintex services back to normal operation. 

Our engineering team will continue monitoring the services health.
This issue has been resolved.

incidents.title: Intermittent errors processing documents in US and Canada

timeline.details: DocGen for Workflow is currently experiencing increased error rates due to an incident with our cloud provider. Engineers are working to resolve the issue as quickly as possible.
Engineers have applied a fix and stabilized the environment.

incidents.title: NA1 and CAN1 Regions - Status Endpoint Issues

timeline.details: Engineers are currently investigating an issue in NA1 and CAN1 affecting the jobs status endpoint.
Engineers have implemented a fix and are monitoring the situation.
Status endpoint issue has been resolved.

incidents.title: DocGen for Workflow NA1 Service Disruption

timeline.details: Engineers are investigating an issue with the NA1 region.
Updating status.
Engineers have identified the cause and mitigated the issue.
The incident is resolved.

We are currently investigating an issue impacting new users accessing Nintex Customer Central for the first time. Our team is actively working to identify the cause and restore normal access. Updates will be provided as they become available.
The team has resolved an issue with the user provisioning API and believes the issue should now be resolved. The team will continue monitoring to ensure the fix is working as expected.
All sign-up issues have now been resolved.

incidents.title: Nintex Workflow - Issue to start workflow and duplicated instances

timeline.details: We found an unable to start workflow in Production NEU. The team is currently investigating the issue
We are currently investigating the issue. Some clients(services) are not receiving responses, which may result in duplicate workflow executions.
The engineering team has identified the underlying cause and currently working on the remediation.
We have identified the issue and have fixed it. The start workflow should run stably for now.
The issue has been resolved, and we are closely monitoring the system for a day.
The start workflow is operating normally following the monitoring period.

incidents.title: Nintex SPFx Forms - License Expired issue.

timeline.details:
We are made aware of the issue where user are getting license expired error when accessing SPFx Form. The team has identified and mitigating the issue.

We  recommend affected users to contact Nintex Support or Licensing Team  for further assistance.

The licensing team has applied remediation steps. 

We recommend customers encountering this issue to raise ticket to Nintex Support team or Licensing team  for further assistance.
The issue is now resolved

incidents.title: Delay in displaying events in the Workflow Instance Details

timeline.details: The Nintex Engineering team has identified and are currently investigating a delay in displaying events in the Workflow Instance Details page. Workflow execution is not affected.
Issue is resolved

incidents.title: Intermittent Task in Production NEU Failing

timeline.details: We have identified an issue with one of the service that affecting Task to fail. We have fix the issue and will be monitoring further.
The issue has been fully resolved & monitored, all services are operating normally and stable.

incidents.title: Nintex DocGen for Salesforce - Invalid Session ID

timeline.details: Engineers are investigating reports of customers encountering an INVALID_SESSION_ID exception

This incident has been resolved and all systems are operational again.

Engineers will continue to monitor the system.
This incident has been resolved and all services are operational.

incidents.title: Nintex Workflow - Issue with AI Xtension Builder

timeline.details:
Our monitoring has indicated that the AI Xtension Builder is currently unavailable. 

Our team have identified the issue and in the process of remediating.
Our team has applied the remediation and the service is back to normal operation. The issue has been resolved.

incidents.title: Issue loading Nintex Forms for Office 365

timeline.details:
We are aware of an issue access Nintex Forms for Office 365

We are currently investigating and will post an update as more information is available.
The engineering team has identified the issue and remediation has been applied. Nintex Engineering will continue to monitor the issue.
The issue is now resolved

We are currently experiencing intermittent issues with email delivery. Our team is actively working with our vendor to identify the cause and restore normal service as quickly as possible. We will provide further updates as more information becomes available.

We are continuing to work closely with our vendor to resolve the intermittent email delivery issues. Investigation is ongoing, and we will provide further updates as more information becomes available.

We continue to work closely with our vendor to investigate and resolve the intermittent email delivery issues. The issue remains under active investigation, and we will provide further updates as progress continues.

We continue to work with our vendor on the intermittent email delivery issues. The issue remains under active investigation, and we are awaiting a resolution. We will provide further updates as soon as more information becomes available.
Intermittent email delivery issues is recovering. The issue remains under monitoring. We will provide further updates as soon as it has been resolved.
The issue affecting email delivery has now been resolved. Services have returned to normal operation. We will continue to monitor to ensure stability.

We are currently experiencing a recurrence of intermittent email delivery issues impacting some workflows. Our team is working closely with our vendor to resolve this as quickly as possible. We will continue to provide updates as we learn more.
Email delivery has been restored and services are operating normally.  We are continuing to monitor the situation closely to ensure ongoing stability.
The issue affecting email delivery has now been resolved. Services have returned to normal operation. We will continue to monitor to ensure stability.
Our telemetry shows that the service has resumed normal operations. Consider resuming/restarting the instance if it unsuccessfully executed during the previous execution.
The issue has been resolved

We are currently investigating an issue where multiple customers are unable to log in to the platform. Affected users encounter the error message “Whoops, that wasn’t meant to happen!” when attempting to access the application.

This issue is impacting users across multiple environments. Our engineering teams are actively working to identify the root cause and restore normal service as quickly as possible.

We’ll provide updates here as more information becomes available.

The issue impacting user logins across multiple environments has been resolved. Users should now be able to access the platform normally without encountering the error message.

Our engineering teams will continue to closely monitor the platform to ensure stability and prevent recurrence.

Thank you for your patience while we worked to restore service.
The login issue has now been fully resolved and authentication is working as expected.

incidents.title: Nintex DocGen for Salesforce - Scheduled DDPs

timeline.details: Engineers are currently investigating an issue affecting some customers where the schedule for Scheduled DDP's cannot be  edited.

Engineers have identified the issue and are working towards a fix.

During this window, should any customer have an urgent requirement to Deactivate a Scheduled DocGen Package, please contact support@nintex.com

Engineers have identified the issue and are working towards a fix.

During this window, should any customer have an urgent requirement to Deactivate a Scheduled DocGen Package, please contact support@nintex.com
This issue has been resolved and all services are fully functional.
